tipRules.tip: Hackney: 'Fish the outside grassline edge where a steep drop swings into it. Hop the jig once or twice then reel it in - in clear water if they are going to get it, they get it really quick.'
Hackney: 'You have to be precise and hit the fish right on the head - they are not coming to get it. Make a lot of casts and be real thorough.'
Hackney: 'Fish the 1-ounce jig, then go to 1.25-ounce to fish faster. Bass only hit it on the initial drop.'

colorRules.color: candy craw jig with junebug trailer
blue craw jig with green pumpkin sapphire trailer

tipRules.tip: Hackney: 'Fish a 1/2-ounce Rage Blade on inside grasslines for shallow fish.'

knowledge.insight: Hackney: 'There were more numbers of fish shallow, but bigger ones were on the deeper edge of that grass.'
Hackney: 'Most guys flipping grass were using a punch skirt and plastic or just plastic. But I am a firm believer that when they are biting the jig, it catches the big ones.'
Hackney: 'Anywhere I caught bass, I had to get perch bites. If I fished a stretch of grass and I did not get any perch or bluegill bites, there would not be any bass there.'
Hackney: 'Flipped with a 7-foot-11 rod, EXO PT 100 reel, and 50-pound braid for deep grass jigging.'
